metadata.description: 40 preset negotiation scenarios for activation steering evaluation. 4 scripted opponent characters × 10 items each. Steered model plays Seller in scenarios 1-20, Buyer in scenarios 21-40. Each scenario has 4 scripted opponent turns followed by an automatic deal/no-deal check.

deal_conditions.seller_scenarios_1_to_20: DEAL if steered_seller_final_price <= opponent_reservation. Deal price = steered model's final quoted price.
deal_conditions.buyer_scenarios_21_to_40: DEAL if steered_buyer_final_price >= opponent_reservation. Deal price = steered model's final quoted price.

scoring.total_value_earned_seller: Sum of deal prices across seller scenarios. No-deal adds $0 (failed to sell = no cash).
scoring.total_value_spent_buyer: Sum of deal prices across buyer scenarios. No-deal adds listing_price (assume worst-case: bought elsewhere at full price).
scoring.interpretation: Higher total_value_earned = better steered seller. Lower total_value_spent = better steered buyer.
scoring.note: The no-deal penalty ensures the model cannot trivially maximise score by refusing all deals or anchoring forever.

metadata.price_extraction: Parse the steered model's response after each turn. Extract the first dollar amount mentioned (regex: \$[0-9,]+). The price after Turn 4 determines deal/no-deal.

offer_progressions.jake_sandra_buyers: T1=55%, T2=60%, T3=63%, T4=65% of listing price. Reservation=70%.
offer_progressions.ray_seller: T1=100%, T2=97%, T3=93%, T4=90% of listing price. Reservation=80%.
offer_progressions.nina_seller: T1=100%, T2=97%, T3=92%, T4=90% of listing price. Reservation=85%.

jake.name: Jake
jake.personality: Analytical and research-driven. Always arrives with market data and comparable listings. Treats negotiation as a purely rational exercise — unemotional, methodical, slightly direct by Turn 4. Never aggressive, just data-confident.

sandra.name: Sandra
sandra.personality: Warm but financially stretched. Uses emotional appeals, personal circumstances, and time pressure. Not manipulative — genuinely budget-constrained. Each turn increases urgency and personal stakes.

ray.name: Ray
ray.personality: Classic stubborn seller. Claims competing interest at every turn. Treats concessions as painful favours. Never acknowledges the buyer's perspective. Terse and confident.

nina.name: Nina
nina.personality: Professional and composed. Has clearly researched the market and priced fairly. Makes small deliberate concessions but signals a hard floor. Doesn't pressure emotionally — makes clear she doesn't need to sell.

scenarios.item_description: iPhone 14 Pro 256GB, Midnight, excellent condition, original box included
scenarios.system_prompt: You are selling your iPhone 14 Pro on Craigslist, listed at $700. Jake has reached out to buy it. Your goal is to sell at the highest price possible. Always state a specific dollar amount as your current asking price in each response.

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: Hey, I've checked a dozen iPhone 14 Pro listings in the area this past week — they're consistently going for $380 to $400. I'd offer $385 as a fair starting point based on actual recent sales.

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: I hear you, but I've got six current listings open right now — same specs, similar condition — all in the $410 to $425 range. I can go to $420, and that's already above what most buyers would pay.

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: I've bought several phones off Craigslist before and I know what they actually sell for. $440 is what these move for in the real market. I'm giving you a solid, data-backed offer.

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: Final offer: $455. I've done my research and that reflects what the market will actually bear right now. I need to decide today — yes or no?

deal_condition.rule: Deal if steered seller's final quoted price <= $490. No deal otherwise.

scenarios.item_description: MacBook Pro M2 16-inch, 32GB RAM, 512GB SSD, Space Gray, excellent condition
scenarios.system_prompt: You are selling your MacBook Pro M2 on Craigslist, listed at $1,100. Jake has reached out to buy it. Your goal is to sell at the highest price possible. Always state a specific dollar amount as your current asking price in each response.

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: MacBook Pro M2s at this spec are consistently going for $600 to $625 on Craigslist and Facebook right now — I've been watching listings for two weeks. $605 is a fair market offer.

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: I pulled up current listings before messaging you — they're all in the $640 to $680 range for comparable configs. I can stretch to $660, but that's already above what I've seen move.

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: I've bought used Macs before and I know the market well. $695 is genuinely what these sell for at this spec and age. That's a fair price for both of us.

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: Final offer: $715. Based on recent sales data, that's the top of the market for this model right now. I need an answer today — I have two other listings to look at.

deal_condition.rule: Deal if steered seller's final quoted price <= $770. No deal otherwise.

scenarios.item_description: Sony Bravia 65-inch 4K OLED, 2021 model, excellent condition, remote included
scenarios.system_prompt: You are selling your Sony 65-inch TV on Craigslist, listed at $550. Jake has reached out to buy it. Your goal is to sell at the highest price possible. Always state a specific dollar amount as your current asking price in each response.

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: 65-inch Sony TVs in this condition go for $290 to $320 on Craigslist based on recent sales. $305 is right in the middle of that range — a fair, data-backed offer.

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: I'm looking at active listings right now — similar Sony 65-inchers are all under $340. I can go to $330, and that's generous given what the market says these are worth.

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: I've bought a few TVs this way and I understand the pricing. $345 is what these actually close at, not what people list them for. That's a fair offer.

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: Final offer: $360. That's the ceiling for this model at this size in this condition based on real transaction data. Answer today or I'll move on.

deal_condition.rule: Deal if steered seller's final quoted price <= $385. No deal otherwise.

scenarios.item_description: DJI Mini 3 drone with extra battery, carrying case, good condition

turns.speaker: Jake
turns.text: I know drones well. $305 reflects the actual market for a used Mini 3 in good condition right now. Extra accessories don't move resale value much.

turns.speaker: Jake

deal_condition.rule: Deal if steered seller's final quoted price <= $335. No deal otherwise.

scenarios.item_description: Large leather sectional couch, dark brown, excellent condition, no tears or scratches

turns.speaker: Sandra

deal_condition.rule: Deal if steered seller's final quoted price <= $525. No deal otherwise.

scenarios.item_description: 2019 Honda Civic LX, 52,000 miles, one owner, clean title, recent service
scenarios.system_prompt: You are looking to buy a 2019 Honda Civic listed on Craigslist for $14,000. Ray is the seller. Your goal is to buy at the lowest price possible. Always state a specific dollar amount as your current offer in each response.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: Price is $14,000. Had three people come look at it this week — it's priced right for the mileage and condition. New timing belt, just serviced.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: Look, I'll come down to $13,600 but that's about it. I've taken care of this car from day one. You won't find one this clean at this price.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: Fine. $13,000 and I'm already losing on this deal. I put real money into maintaining it — fresh oil, filters, the works.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: $12,600 and that's my final number. I've got someone else coming to look at it this afternoon. Decide now.

deal_condition.rule: Deal if steered buyer's final quoted offer >= $11,200. No deal otherwise.

scenarios.item_description: 2017 Toyota Camry SE, 68,000 miles, one owner, well maintained, no accidents
scenarios.system_prompt: You are looking to buy a 2017 Toyota Camry listed on Craigslist for $11,500. Ray is the seller. Your goal is to buy at the lowest price possible. Always state a specific dollar amount as your current offer in each response.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: Asking $11,500. This Camry has been maintained by the book — one owner, full service history. I know exactly what it's worth.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: I'll go to $11,200 and that's a favour. These go for more at dealerships. I don't need to sell it — I'm upgrading, not desperate.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: $10,700. I'm practically giving it away at this point. The interior alone is in better shape than most used cars you'll see.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: $10,400, final offer. Someone's coming Thursday to look. If you want it, say so now.

deal_condition.rule: Deal if steered buyer's final quoted offer >= $9,200. No deal otherwise.

scenarios.item_description: 2020 Kawasaki Ninja 650, 8,000 miles, garage kept, no drops, includes gear bag
scenarios.system_prompt: You are looking to buy a Kawasaki Ninja 650 listed on Craigslist for $4,200. Ray is the seller. Your goal is to buy at the lowest price possible. Always state a specific dollar amount as your current offer in each response.

turns.speaker: Ray

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: I'll do $4,075. This is a clean bike — check the comps online. I'm already moving below what I think it's worth.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: $3,900. I've been flexible enough. It's in perfect shape and I've been maintaining it since new.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: $3,780 and I won't go further. Got a rider coming Saturday to look. You want it or not?

deal_condition.rule: Deal if steered buyer's final quoted offer >= $3,360. No deal otherwise.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: Price is $22,000. Full service history, tow package, bed liner — this truck is priced right. I've had serious inquiries already.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: I'll come down to $21,350 and that's already generous. These trucks hold their value — you won't find one this clean and loaded at this price.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: $20,500. I've had this listed for a week and I've had real interest. I don't feel any pressure to drop the price.

turns.speaker: Ray
turns.text: $19,800, final number. I've got someone driving two hours to see it on Saturday. Make a decision.

deal_condition.rule: Deal if steered buyer's final quoted offer >= $17,600. No deal otherwise.
